title: Reliable Bash Automation and Tests · Terminal Trace: Bash Temporary Cleanup
summary: Turn a fragile command history into a bounded script that validates inputs, preserves data, reports failures, and can be rerun safely. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux bash temporary cleanup increment in a tested idempotent Bash operations script. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create temporary resources safely and guarantee cleanup across success, expected failure, signals, and partial work.
Handle positional parameters, defaults, validation, arrays, and quoted expansion without word splitting or glob injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.
